claims.text: Signed SB 300, the Heartbeat Protection Act, prohibiting abortion after detection of fetal cardiac activity (approximately 6 weeks). Became Chapter 2023-21 of Florida law on April 14, 2023.
Florida's FY 2021–2023 budgets excluded state funding for elective abortion providers; DeSantis-era administration prioritized alternatives-to-abortion programs over reproductive-health clinics.

Signed CS/CS/HB 1557, 'Parental Rights in Education,' restricting classroom instruction on sexual orientation and gender identity in K–3 (and requiring age-appropriate limits thereafter). Became Chapter 2022-22 on March 28, 2022; effective July 1, 2022.

Signed CS/HB 543, 'Public Safety,' permitting unlicensed concealed carry of firearms for law-abiding citizens (permitless / 'constitutional carry'). Approved April 3, 2023; Chapter 2023-18; effective July 1, 2023.

Signed SB 1718 (2023), strengthening illegal-immigration enforcement: expanded E-Verify mandate for employers of 25+, criminalized transportation of illegal aliens into Florida, and invalidated out-of-state driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

Signed HB 1 (2023), making Florida the first universal-school-choice state: Empowerment Scholarship Accounts (ESAs) eligibility opened to all K-12 students regardless of public-school enrollment or family income. Approved March 27, 2023.

Signed HB 7 (2022), the 'Individual Freedom' / 'Stop WOKE' Act, restricting instruction that compels students or employees to endorse specific race/sex-based concepts. Became Chapter 2022-72 on April 22, 2022.

Signed SB 90 (2021) tightening Florida's election-integrity regime: constraints on mail-ballot drop boxes, stricter voter-ID requirements at ballot-request stage, and limits on third-party ballot collection.

claims.text: Planned Parenthood Action 0%.

Co-introduced the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (2024) with Sen. Ted Cruz to prohibit the Federal Reserve from issuing a central bank digital currency directly or indirectly to individuals, calling a CBDC 'a massive overreach' and 'non-starter.' In May 2026 reintroduced the Chinese CBDC Prohibition Act barring money services businesses and the U.S. Postal Service from using any China-issued digital currency — opposing government financial surveillance on both fronts.

In May 2025 reintroduced a Federal Reserve accountability legislative package to end the Fed's 'out-of-control monetary policies' and restore sound money discipline; separately backed a Principles-Based Balanced Budget Constitutional Amendment (December 2025) requiring the federal government to balance its budget — consistent with the rubric's sound-money and anti-deficit standards.

candidates.name: Ashley Moody
candidates.notes: Appointed to U.S. Senate January 2025 by Gov. DeSantis to fill Marco Rubio's seat after Rubio's confirmation as Secretary of State. As AG: led legal challenges to Biden immigration policy, joined multi-state suits against federal vaccine mandates, defended FL Parental Rights in Education Act. In Senate: voted YES on Hegseth, Gabbard, Bondi, and other Trump 2025 cabinet nominations. NRA A rating.

claims.text: Cosponsored the Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act (2025), endorsed by the NRA, Gun Owners of America, and NSSF, which would allow law-abiding citizens with home-state carry permits to carry in any reciprocating state — opposing new federal firearm restrictions.

Sworn in as Senator with an explicit agenda of supporting President Trump's border-security measures including wall construction and military deployment at the southern border; described border enforcement as a top legislative priority.

Sponsored S.3650 (January 2026) to prohibit transporting minors across state lines to circumvent parental-involvement laws in abortion decisions, reinforcing parental authority over minor children's medical decisions.

candidates.name: Jimmy Patronis
candidates.notes: Won the 4/1/2025 FL-01 special election (Gaetz seat) 57-42; former Florida CFO 2017-2025; running for reelection 2026 (primary 8/18/2026). Heritage Action 89-90% (119th). Roll calls all party-line conservative: YEA SAVE Act (Roll 102), YEA One Big Beautiful Bill both passages (Rolls 145/190 — included PP Medicaid defund + $175B border/wall), YEA Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (Roll 201), NAY Iran War Powers Resolution (Roll 199, 6/3/2026 — backed the executive, scored contrary on the war-powers cell). As CFO: divested $2B in state funds from BlackRock over ESG (Dec 2022); fought the OSHA COVID vaccine mandate; vocal CBDC opponent. NRA A+ ('only one in the race with an A+'). Stated pro-life position includes rape/incest/life exceptions (aggregator-sourced), so the personhood cell is left null. Sponsored H.R. 9009 Firearm Freedom Act (5/2026) and H.R. 7393 (529s for homebuying). No cross-party deviations found on any roll call checked; Heritage's ~10% gap implies minor dissents not yet identified.

candidates.name: Neal Dunn
candidates.notes: Physician (urologist, ex-Army surgeon); FL-02 since 2017; Vice Chair, Energy & Commerce (July 2025). CURRENCY: announced 2026-01-13 he will not seek reelection ('pass the torch'); still holds the seat as of 6/2026 amid reported health concerns; no run for other office. Conservative roll-call record: YEA Pain-Capable (Roll 549/2017), YEA Born-Alive (Roll 29/2023), NAY Respect for Marriage Act both votes (Rolls 373+513/2022), NAY Equality Act twice (217/2019, 39/2021), YEA Women & Girls in Sports (12/2025), AYE Parents Bill of Rights (161/2023), AYE Anti-CBDC (230/2024), YEA SAVE Act twice (345/2024, 102/2025), NAY For the People Act (62/2021), YEA Secure the Border Act H.R.2 (209/2023), YEA Laken Riley (23/2025), AYE Concealed Carry Reciprocity (663/2017), NAY post-Uvalde Safer Communities Act (299/2022), objected to AZ+PA electoral certification (Rolls 10+11, Jan 2021). DEVIATIONS: YEA $61B Ukraine supplemental (Roll 151, 4/2024 — 112 Rs voted no); AYE Fiscal Responsibility Act debt deal (243/2023, Heritage-opposed); NAY 2002-AUMF repeal (172/2021). candidates.name: Randy Fine
candidates.notes: FL-06 since the April 2025 special election (replacing Mike Waltz); FL House 2016-2023, FL Senate 2024-2025; House Foreign Affairs. Self-styled 'Hebrew Hammer' — authored FL's IHRA antisemitism-definition law (HB 741, 2019, signed in Jerusalem). DEVIATIONS: supported FL SB 7026 (2018 red-flag + age-21 law), telling NPR he'd 'absolutely' accept a lower NRA grade — later filed a bill repealing the under-21 purchase ban; ~$458K in AIPAC-network money flooded his 2025 special (Evanston Now/Sludge). Strong FL record on minors' gender-care ban (sponsor), anti-drag law, HB 1 universal vouchers (co-introducer), HB 543 permitless carry (co-introducer), school moment-of-silence law (sponsor). Abortion record is restriction-tier (sponsored the 15-week ban, yea on the 6-week ban) — no documented abolition stance. Hawkish-interventionist posture (Greenland Annexation and Statehood Act, Jan 2026) noted; opposed one Ukraine aid bill on the floor. Heritage Action: no session score yet at research time.

claims.text: Voted yes on the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R. 26, 118th Congress, Jan 11 2023), which requires medical care for infants born alive after a failed abortion. The bill passed the House 220-210.

Cosponsored the Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act (H.R. 38, 119th Congress, 2025) — legislation requiring all states to honor concealed-carry permits issued by other states, an expansion of carry rights consistent with constitutional carry principles.

Cosponsored the Birthright Citizenship Act of 2025 (H.R. 569, 119th Congress) — a bill to end automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents who are neither citizens nor lawful permanent residents.

candidates.name: Mike Haridopolos
candidates.notes: FL-08 (Space Coast) since 2025 (replacing Bill Posey); FL Senate President 2010-2012. Thin federal record — scored from 119th-Congress marquee roll calls (verified in Clerk XML) and cosponsor lists; many cells honestly null. As Senate President, credited by Florida Family Action for the 2011 ultrasound law's passage.

candidates.name: Maxwell Frost
candidates.notes: FL-10 (Orlando) since 2023; youngest House member when elected (b. 1997, Afro-Cuban); former March for Our Lives national organizing director — gun control is his signature issue (platform: assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, 'dismantle the NRA'; Giffords endorsed; Everytown-endorsed gun-violence research bill). Progressive on life (Born-Alive nay 2023 + 2025, NARAL 100%), gender (HR 734 + HR 28 women's-sports nays, Equality Act cosponsor), elections (SAVE nay twice, also NO on banning noncitizen voting in DC), border (HR 2 + Laken Riley nays). Took office after the RFMA vote (12/2022) — no marriage roll call. DEVIATIONS: YEA on H.Res 894 declaring anti-Zionism antisemitism while 92 Dems voted present (Roll 697, 12/2023); Heritage Action 26% in the 118th, unusually high for a progressive; AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal. Voted NAY on the April 2024 Israel supplemental (yea on Ukraine); listed by Track AIPAC as rejecting AIPAC support (unverified).

candidates.name: Daniel Webster
candidates.notes: FL-11 (The Villages) since 2011; former FL House Speaker (1996-98, first GOP speaker since Reconstruction). Southern Baptist (First Baptist Church of Central Florida). RETIRING: announced 2026-04-28 — 'time has come to pass the torch'; seat open 2026. Strong social-conservative record: Life at Conception Act cosponsor, Born-Alive reintroducer, SBA A+; NO on Respect for Marriage Act and Equality Act (own opposition statement); original cosponsor + yes on the women's-sports bill; authored the My Child, My Choice Act (parental consent on SOGI instruction). SAVE Act yes (2024) + SAVE America Act yes (2026); HR 2 + Laken Riley yes. Maverick streak: ran for Speaker against leadership three times (2015 vs Boehner, Oct 2015 with Freedom Caucus backing, 2017); NO on the Dec 2022 omnibus; NO on the April 2024 Ukraine supplemental ('blank check') — but AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal. Voted to pass the Balanced Budget Amendment (HJRes 139, 3/2026). NRA A. AIPAC gave $49,550 in the 2023-24 cycle (OpenSecrets). Heritage 94% (117th), lifetime ~78%.

claims.text: Carries 100% rating from SBA Pro-Life America; voted consistently to protect life and defund Big Abortion businesses including Planned Parenthood of Medicaid dollars via H.R.1 One Big Beautiful Bill (July 2025).

Former Florida Secretary of State (2018–2022) who built the state's election-security infrastructure; introduced a constitutional amendment in the 119th Congress (2025) restricting federal voting eligibility to U.S. citizens only.

candidates.name: Vern Buchanan
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] RETIRING: announced 1/27/2026 he will not seek reelection; serving out his term (FL-16 open seat). Business-Republican moderate — Heritage Action lifetime 64% (as low as 41% in the 115th). Solid pro-life restriction votes (Nay WHPA x2, Yea Born-Alive 2025, Nay embryonic stem-cell 2007) but never a Life at Conception cosponsor in 3 straight Congresses. Nay on Respect for Marriage Act BOTH votes (July + Dec 2022), Nay Equality Act. KEY CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (guns): Yea on HR 8 universal background checks in 2019 AND 2021 (one of only 8 Republicans in 2021); 2019 letter to McConnell urging passage of background checks and consideration of red-flag laws; early bump-stock-ban advocate; NRA grade dropped A to B by 2020 — yet held the line Nay on the 2022 AWB, federal ERPO, and Safer Communities Act. Also: Yea on the \$61B Ukraine supplemental; voted to certify the 2020 election (Nay on both Jan 6 objections); Balanced Budget Amendment is his first bill every Congress since 2007.

profile.religion: Baptist (Pew religious-composition survey of Congress)
profile.background: US Representative since 2007 (FL-13, then FL-16). Vice Chair, Ways & Means; Chair, W&M Health Subcommittee. Businessman. Announced Jan 27, 2026 he will not seek reelection — FL-16 open in 2026.

Life at Conception Act cosponsor in three straight Congresses with a personhood op-ed ('unborn children are entitled to the full protections of life under the law'); Nay twice on the Respect for Marriage Act; THE sponsor of the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (HR 734/HR 28); SCREEN Act (porn age-verification) cosponsor x2; Audit-the-Fed cosponsor x2; Nay on the FRA debt deal; SAVE Act cosponsor; HR 2 + Laken Riley Yea; Birthright Citizenship Act cosponsor; original cosponsor of the Constitutional Hearing Protection Act (suppressors off the NFA); led legislation to reinstate troops discharged over the COVID vaccine mandate; GRACE Act sponsor (religious exemptions from school vaccine mandates). America-First: Nay on the \$60B Ukraine supplemental, Yea on Israel aid, cosponsor of No WHO Pandemic Treaty Without Senate Approval. CROSS-PARTY NOTE: Yea to repeal the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021, one of ~49 Rs). Missed Feb 2023 votes (incl. anti-ESG CRA) recovering from a serious ladder fall — absence, not position.

claims.text: Tracked by SBA Pro-Life America with a consistent pro-life voting record; cosponsored the Pain-Capable Unborn Children from Late-Term Abortions Act; voted against federal abortion funding and spoke against Florida's all-trimester abortion ballot amendment — affirming protection of the unborn aligned with the rubric's life-at-conception standard.

Voted against H.R.8 (expanded universal background checks) and H.R.1446 (which would allow FBI to delay firearm transfers indefinitely); formally opposed H.R.2377, the Federal Extreme Risk Protection Order Act (red-flag law), in the House Judiciary Committee — directly matching the rubric's rejection of background-check expansions, red-flag laws, and magazine restrictions.

candidates.name: Scott Franklin
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Reliable party-line conservative with a Navy-establishment streak. Nay on the Respect for Marriage Act both votes — but his own statement was federalism/conscience-framed and said he has no issue with government recognizing 'civil unions between any two persons' (biblical_marriage q1 FALSE: documented openness to civil unions). Life at Conception Act cosponsor in the 117th and 118th but dropped in the 119th; Yea Born-Alive, women's-sports x2, HR 2, Laken Riley x3, SAVE x2, CBDC ban, pistol-brace CRA; ACE Act + Audit-the-Fed + Birthright Citizenship Act cosponsor; NRA A/Endorsed (2024); elder at First Presbyterian Lakeland; voted to sustain the 2020 AZ/PA elector objections. DEVIATIONS (intra-coalition): Aye on the Fiscal Responsibility Act debt deal (Heritage 118th score 70%); Nay on 2024 Ukraine aid but anti-restraint on war powers (Nay AUMF repeal, Nay Syria withdrawal); AIPAC \$10k reported (unverified compilation). Silent on IVF, euthanasia, raw milk, Pentagon audit.

candidates.notes: Army combat veteran (lost both legs in Afghanistan). Chair, House Foreign Affairs Committee (119th). Heritage Action 84% session / 77% lifetime. SBA Pro-Life A+; Life at Conception Act cosponsor but holds rape/incest/life exceptions (restrictionist, not abolitionist). KEY DEVIATIONS: 2018 post-Parkland NYT op-ed called for banning future sales of 'assault or tactical firearms' + universal background checks — NRA downgraded him to 'D' (2022); no retraction found. One of Congress's largest AIPAC recipients; opposed June 2026 Iran war-powers resolution as HFAC chair. Jan 2021: objected to AZ/PA electors. claims.text: Holds a 100% pro-life rating from the National Right to Life Committee; stated 'The decision to overturn Roe v. Wade will protect millions of innocent babies' and voted for the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.26), consistently opposing taxpayer abortion funding.

Has publicly committed to 'fight to protect our Second Amendment from all attempts to erode it'; helped pass concealed carry reciprocity legislation during his House tenure and opposed all measures to restrict lawful firearm ownership.

Voted NO on the $95 billion Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan foreign-aid supplemental package in April 2024, opposing open-ended overseas military entanglements; as House Foreign Affairs Committee Chair from January 2025, insists on 'America First' accountability before any foreign aid flows.

candidates.name: Lois Frankel
candidates.notes: Progressive Democrat; lead House sponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act (nationwide abortion access) and twice voted Nay on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act. Equality Act cosponsor/Yea; Yea Respect for Marriage Act (Dec 2022); twice Nay on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act; Nay Parents Bill of Rights. Nay SAVE Act (2024+2025); Yea H.R. 1 (For the People). Nay Laken Riley Act (twice), Nay H.R. 30, Nay H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act. Yea 2022 assault-weapons ban; No on 2017 concealed-carry reciprocity; NRA F. EMILY's List was her #1 contributor (2012, $51,456); AIPAC/Israel-lobby career total $820,719 (TrackAIPAC) — AIPAC her #1 org contributor in 2022/2024/2026 cycles. Solid on life/elections/marquee votes: SBA Pro-Life A+, Yea Born-Alive, Nay WHPA, Nay Equality Act, Yea women's-sports protection (HR 734), Yea Parents Bill of Rights, Yea SAVE Act, Nay HR 1, Yea HR 2, Yea Laken Riley, Yea CBDC ban, Nay 2022 AWB. Heritage Action 51-53% recent sessions — NOT the 75-85% previously recorded. KEY DEVIATIONS: NRA grade 'D' (2022 — 'usually supports restrictive gun control,' not endorsed); lead sponsor of DIGNIDAD Act (legal-status pathway for undocumented + Dreamer LPR path + mandatory E-Verify) and publicly fought Trump-era deportations of Venezuelan TPS/parole holders; Yea on RFMA July 2022 before Dec flip to Nay over religious-liberty protections; supports rape/incest exceptions post-Dobbs (restrictionist, not abolitionist). Foreign policy least restrained: Yea $61B Ukraine supplemental, $10K AIPAC PAC (2023-24), Cuba/Venezuela maximum-pressure hawk.

profile.religion: Protestant (per Pew 119th Congress list; prior 'Catholic' assumption unsettled)
profile.background: US Representative FL-27 since 2021. Miami-born daughter of Cuban exiles; 5-time Emmy-winning broadcast journalist (Univision/Telemundo/CNN Español). B.A. Communications, University of Miami; MPA, Harvard Kennedy School. Chairs Western Hemisphere subcommittee. Heritage Action 51% (119th), ~57-63% lifetime — below prior 75-85% estimate.
profile.office: 2162 Rayburn House Office Building

claims.text: Holds 100% rating from SBA Pro-Life America and National Right to Life Committee; voted for One Big Beautiful Bill (H.R.1, July 2025), the first federal law to defund Planned Parenthood of Medicaid dollars.
Voted YES on H.R. 8281 — Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act (April 2025), requiring documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote in federal elections.

Joined 7 other Republicans in voting for the Bipartisan Background Checks Act of 2021 (H.R.8), expanding federal background-check requirements to all commercial firearm transfers — contrary to the rubric's opposition to new gun restrictions.

claims.text: Holds a pro-life voting record tracked by SBA Pro-Life America's National Pro-Life Scorecard; voted to defend the lives of the unborn and infants and actively opposed Florida's 2024 abortion expansion amendment (Amendment 4).

In April 2026, was one of only six House Republicans to vote with all Democrats to preserve Temporary Protected Status for approximately 350,000 Haitian immigrants despite the Trump administration's efforts to terminate TPS — siding with a stay-of-deportation rather than mandatory removal.

As Miami-Dade County Mayor, ordered his corrections department to honor all ICE detainer requests; the Miami-Dade County Board of Commissioners formally codified this anti-sanctuary policy by a 9-to-3 vote, directly rejecting the sanctuary-city model.

candidates.name: Jay Trumbull
candidates.office: U.S. Representative FL-02 (2026 R Candidate · FL state senator · Dunn-seat open)
candidates.notes: Florida state senator (Panama City area, since 2022). Former FL House. R primary candidate for FL-02 open seat (Neal Dunn retiring).

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — open-seat successor placeholder (FL-2). Archetype: maga_conservative_r.

claims.text: Voted YES on Florida SB 300 (2023), the six-week abortion ban signed by Gov. DeSantis and effective May 1, 2024, as confirmed by the Florida Senate roll call — casting a vote to protect unborn life from heartbeat detection.

Voted YES on Florida SB 1718 (2023) — the comprehensive immigration enforcement law mandating E-Verify for private employers, state-local cooperation on federal immigration detainers, and new transport penalties for unlawfully present persons — confirmed YES in committee (14-6) and on the Senate floor (27-10).

Voted YES on Florida HB 991 (2026), the Florida SAVE Act requiring proof of U.S. citizenship for voter registration; the Senate passed it 27-12 with only one Republican (Sen. Calatayud) dissenting, making Trumbull part of the near-unanimous Republican majority backing the measure.

candidates.notes: Elected Florida Senate President for the 2024–2026 term. Republican from central Florida (FL Senate District 27, covering Polk, Hardee, Highlands, and DeSoto counties plus parts of Okeechobee and Osceola). Former Florida House member. Strong conservative on agricultural policy, fiscal restraint, and Christian heritage themes. Signed onto priority legislation on property tax relief, hurricane recovery, and education accountability during his presidency. President of the Florida Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Florida_Senate.

candidates.name: Eileen Higgins
candidates.notes: 44th Mayor of Miami (D); won Dec 9 2025 runoff 59.46% vs. Emilio Gonzalez 40.54%; sworn in Dec 18 2025; term to 2029. First woman Mayor of Miami; first non-Hispanic since 1996; first Democrat elected mayor since 1997. Former Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 5 (Jun 2018-Nov 2025; resigned to take mayoral office). Former U.S. State Dept FSO and Peace Corps Country Director (Belize). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] HARD evidence (county tenure + mayoral campaign): SPONSORED 2019 Miami-Dade ordinance creating county's first codified LGBTQ Advisory Board (incl. explicit trans rep); declared countywide Transgender Day of Visibility 2024; multi-year Pride proclamation/flag-raising history at County Hall — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE and family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. HARD evidence on guns: longtime Moms Demand Action volunteer; Giffords-endorsed; advocated background-check expansion and against gun shows on county property — self_defense q0/q1 FALSE. HARD evidence on immigration: publicly opposes Miami's 287(g) cooperation, vowed to minimize Miami PD ICE involvement, called Alligator Alcatraz 'a sin'; quoted: 'There's no reason police in the city of Miami should be doing the job of federal law enforcement' — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE and refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE (asserted local control against state ICE mandate, content-neutral on direction). NO direct quote located on personhood-from-conception or abortion-abolition (Dem-coalition endorsements are indirect — held null per evidence-first discipline). NO documented position on paper ballots / voter ID / Zuckerbucks specifically — null. Religion: Catholic per secondary biographical aggregators; not confirmed via her own statement — left null in profile.

profile.background: Born June 30 1964, Dayton OH; raised Albuquerque NM. BS Mechanical Engineering (U. New Mexico), MBA (Cornell 1989). Former U.S. State Department Foreign Service Officer (Mexico, South Africa) and Peace Corps Country Director (Belize, 2006). Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 5 Jun 2018-Nov 2025 (won 2018 special election after Bruno Barreiro resigned). Elected 44th Mayor of Miami Dec 9 2025 runoff (59.46% vs. Emilio Gonzalez 40.54%); sworn in Dec 18 2025; term to 2029. First woman Mayor of Miami; first non-Hispanic since 1996; first Democrat elected mayor since 1997.

candidates.name: Miguel Angel Gabela
candidates.office: City Commissioner, District 1, City of Miami, Florida (sworn in Dec 2, 2023 · term to Nov 2027 · 2023 runoff winner ~54% vs Alex Diaz de la Portilla)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 1 Commissioner of Miami as of June 2026 (miami.gov bio + Floridian Press Feb 2026 + Miami Today Dec 2025 coverage). Sweep of miamigov.com, Miami Herald / New Times, NBC6/WPLG/WSVN, WLRN, Floridian Press, his campaign site (gabela4miami.com), Ballotpedia. HARD evidence cited: economic_stewardship q2 TRUE — Gabela + Pardo pushed $25M tax cut Dec 2023 after state ruled original budget illegal, plus proposed suspending recycling to save $2.5M in FY25-26 (affirmative restraint posture beyond routine vote). public_justice q0 TRUE — campaign 5-point plan explicitly called for 'additional police staff, enhanced bike and foot patrols' + 'stronger police presence' at Sewell Park (affirmative advocacy, not just budget). border_immigration q2 TRUE — voted YES June 2025 on Miami 287(g) ICE deputization agreement (3-2). refuse_state_overreach q0 FALSE — justified the 287(g) vote as 'We signed this to stay within the law in Florida, ensuring we don't lose critical funds' (Cuba Headlines), explicit COMPLIANCE-with-state-funding rationale not local-control resistance. All other 26 cells null after exhaustive sweep — no public statements located on life, marriage, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, broader public-justice cells (rogue prosecutors / victim restitution / 'public health' framing / Rom 13), subsidiarity, or 2A. NO Pride proclamation / drag-library / CRT vote record at city level. NOT-SCORED note: agent suggested refuse_state_overreach q4 'sideways TRUE' for his NO vote against the unconstitutional term-extension city ord — held NULL because that was refusing a CITY overreach not a STATE directive (strict rubric reading).

profile.religion: No public faith profession located
profile.background: Miguel Angel Gabela — Cuban-born (immigrated to U.S. at age 6, 1970). Auto-parts business owner ~40 years in District 1 with real-estate investments. Married to Mariela for 36 years; two daughters, one grandchild. Prior service on City of Miami Zoning Board. Lost 2019 D1 race, won 2023 D1 runoff against Alex Diaz de la Portilla (~54%). District covers Flagami, Allapattah, parts of Little Havana. One of two NO votes on the Commission's attempted ordinance to push elections from odd to even years (extending sitting terms ~1 yr); appeals court later struck the ordinance down as unconstitutional.

candidates.notes:
Miami City Commission District 2.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement evidence-pass] First openly gay Miami City Commissioner (Nov 2023). SAVE Dade founding Chair (1993) — direct documentary evidence of LGBTQ-rights advocacy supplies all three biblical_marriage cells as FALSE on his OWN public record (not party inference). JUNE 2025: voted NO on Miami's 287(g) ICE partnership, quoted invoking local-control / 'not our brand' language — refuse_state_overreach q0/q2/q4 TRUE; border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE (pro-sanctuary). profile.background: Financial advisor; longtime LGBTQ-rights activist. Founding Chair of SAVE (Safeguarding American Values for Everyone, aka SAVE Dade) in 1993 — the org that successfully lobbied to restore Miami-Dade's gay-rights anti-discrimination ordinance in 1998 after the 1977 Anita Bryant repeal. Co-founded 4Ward Miami (Gay8 Festival); led CareResource (AIDS service org); served on Miami-Dade Gay & Lesbian Chamber. Raised Catholic (St. Hugh + Belen Jesuit Prep); current faith identity not publicly documented. Nov 21 2023 won runoff vs. Sabina Covo; sworn in Dec 2 2023 to 4-year term ending 2027 (postponement that would have pushed term to 2028 was struck down by FL 3rd DCA Aug 2025); first openly gay person elected to Miami City Commission.

candidates.name: Rolando Escalona
candidates.office: City Commissioner, District 3, City of Miami, Florida (sworn in Dec 17, 2025 · 4-yr term to 2029 · won Dec 9, 2025 runoff ~53% vs Frank Carollo)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 3 Commissioner of Miami as of June 2026 (miami.gov D3 bio + CBS swearing-in + Political Cortadito Dec 18 2025). CORRECTION TO PRIOR PROMPT PREMISE: Escalona was NOT a Carollo aide and was NOT appointed in early 2025 — he is a political newcomer (Sexy Fish GM + real-estate broker) who beat Frank Carollo in the Dec 9 2025 runoff. Sweep of miamigov.com, Miami Herald / New Times, NBC6, CBS Miami, WLRN, Florida Politics, Political Cortadito, Calle Ocho News, Miami Today, AOL/Herald op-ed, VFAF endorsement, Hy-Lo News. HARD evidence cited: public_justice q0 TRUE — co-sponsored $450M 'Safe and Ready Miami' public-safety bond going to voters Aug 2026 with on-record statement 'This bond is an investment that reflects my commitment to making public safety a top priority'; campaign promise to hire more officers + restore street patrols (affirmative advocacy bar cleared). border_immigration q2 FALSE — R-DEVIATION FLAG — per Miami Herald (cited in New Times Aug 2026 op-ed + AOL/Herald op-ed) Escalona publicly stated he 'would consider cancelling the city's 287(g) ICE agreement if another commissioner proposed it,' openness to anti-cooperation contrary to sanctuary-opposition cell. All other 28 cells null after sweep — no statements located on life, marriage (no Pride/drag vote on record; 'Little Havana Pride' is neighborhood-pride NOT LGBT Pride per WLRN), family/schools (out of city-comm jurisdiction), Christian liberty, election integrity, broader public-justice cells, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A (FL constitutional carry is state law, no Miami gun-ordinance vote on record). economic_stewardship q2 held NULL — $450M Safe & Ready bond IS deficit financing but campaigned 'lower taxes for seniors'; mixed signal pending a millage vote. Hire of LGBTQ+ Advisory Board chair Trueba as staff signals openness but is not a vote → biblical_marriage cells stay null per no-overread rule.

profile.religion: No public faith profession located
profile.background: Rolando Escalona — born in Havana, Cuba; immigrated to Miami ~2014. Restaurant GM at Brickell's Sexy Fish (157 employees); licensed real-estate broker. Education: Miami Dade College → Florida International University (political science / international relations). Married, twin children. First elected office. Won Dec 9, 2025 D3 runoff with ~53% against Frank Carollo (brother of predecessor Joe Carollo) — political-newcomer outsider beating the Carollo dynasty for the seat. Sworn in Dec 17, 2025; 4-year term through 2029. Endorsed by Veterans For America First (VFAF); also endorsed by his prior-round opponents Alejandro + Piper before the runoff. District 3 covers Little Havana, East Shenandoah, West Brickell, parts of Silver Bluff/The Roads. Hired Ariel Trueba (former 2-term chair of Miami-Dade LGBTQ+ Advisory Board) as staff — signals openness, not an affirmative trans/LGBTQ statement either way.

candidates.name: Suzy Lopez
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Hillsborough SA13 since Jan 7, 2025 full term (DeSantis-appointed Aug 2022; won 52.7% Nov 2024 vs. Warren), term to Jan 2029. HARD evidence: (1) sanctity_of_life q1 TRUE — campaigned specifically on willingness to enforce FL abortion law that predecessor Warren refused; Warren's non-prosecution pledge was the trigger for DeSantis's 2022 suspension and Lopez's appointment (Fox 13, WMNF). (2) biblical_marriage q2 TRUE (indirect) — Warren also pledged not to prosecute gender-affirming care cases for minors; Lopez's appointment was explicitly tied to enforcing FL law as written (WUTV/Sinclair). (3) christian_liberty q0 TRUE — lifelong member of St. John's Episcopal Church Tampa, former Junior Warden of the Vestry (Osprey Observer profile) — public Christian affiliation + lay leadership; note Episcopal tradition is not evangelical-conservative on doctrine, so other Christian-liberty cells (q1-q4) remain null. (4) public_justice q0 TRUE (HIGH BAR met) — endorsed by FL PBA, Tampa PBA, Sun Coast PBA, West Central PBA, Tampa Bay Area Chiefs of Police Assn; personally champions strong PD partnerships via 'Enforce The Law' advisory board. (5) public_justice q1 TRUE — personally meets with homicide victims' families; 'crime victim advocacy should always remain in the forefront.' (6) public_justice q2 TRUE — filed pretrial-detention motions seeking no-bond hold for violent defendants (Foolio murder case). (7) public_justice q3 TRUE — aggressive fentanyl-trafficking prosecution; office stated it will 'vigorously prosecute drug dealers'; cited 1,300% jump in fentanyl cases. (8) public_justice q4 TRUE — sought death penalty within 24 hrs of appointment (Matthew Terry); sought DP repeatedly (Dover double-murder, dismemberment case). (9) border_immigration q2 TRUE — office lists fighting illegal immigration as a focus area; joined Protecting Americans Action Fund pro-enforcement coalition. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood (q0), marriage definition (q0), trans-specific statement (q2), Pride engagement (q4), school choice / parental notification / SOGI K-12 (not SA jurisdiction), christian_liberty q1-q4, economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach (her stance is pro-state-law-enforcement, not resistance), border q1/q3, self_defense q0-q4 — those cells null. DEVIATION-FLAG: she explicitly self-frames as 'a prosecutor, not a politician' — limits how many policy cells fill TRUE.

candidates.name: Donna Deegan
candidates.office: Mayor of Jacksonville (sitting · filed for 2027 re-election Apr 16, 2026)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Mayor of Jacksonville (sworn July 1, 2023). Filed for re-election April 16, 2026; primary March 9, 2027. Catholic (Bishop Kenny HS alumna). HARD evidence (cited): publicly pro-choice (CNN Lead appearance May 2024 opposing FL's 6-week ban; endorsed in 2023 by EMILY's List and Ruth's List, both abortion-rights PACs) — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE. First Jax mayor to issue an LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation (2024), flew Pride flag over City Hall, signed EO creating Mayor's LGBTQ+ Advisory Board, marched as grand marshal in River City Pride — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. Publicly opposed Gov. DeSantis's property-tax constitutional amendment as Tallahassee 'taking control away from local governments'; the 2023 mayoral race was nationally framed as a rebuke of the DeSantis playbook (refuse_state_overreach q0/q3 TRUE — affirmative local-control / subsidiarity posture). Vetoed City Council immigration bill (Ord. 2025-0138) blocking city funds from serving undocumented immigrants ('political theater'); let an earlier immigration-jail ordinance take effect WITHOUT her signature; Hispanic Outreach Director flagged ICE locations on Instagram and Deegan reinstated her saying it was 'not illegal'; FL AG James Uthmeier issued a criminal subpoena to her office Feb 2026 alleging coordination to impede federal immigration enforcement — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE. City building gun registry (logbook of CCW holders' names/firearms entering city buildings) operated under her administration July 2023–2024; FL AG sued Jacksonville for $5M in May 2026 alleging knowing maintenance of an illegal registry; she attended White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention launch Sept 2023 — self_defense q0/q1 FALSE. FY26 budget funded JSO at $638M plus $387M JFRD with $100.1M for first-responder salary/pension increases — robust budgetary support of LE, but per the FXBG rubric high-bar this is routine governance, not the affirmative public 'Back the Blue' advocacy public_justice q0 requires, so that cell is left null. Economic stewardship q2 (balanced-budget orientation): mixed evidence — FY26 held millage flat with zero reserves drawdown and stated intent to pivot to pay-as-you-go, but FY25 drew $47.2M from reserves — null pending clearer pattern. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on Christian liberty, election integrity, family/schools (city does not run schools), or specific public-justice sub-questions — those cells null, not penalized.

HARD evidence (cited): As Finance Vice Chair (2025-26), voted YES on the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (passed 10-9 Sept 23, 2025), publicly stating '$1 in the hands of an individual is better than $1 in the hands of the government' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (affirmative fiscal-restraint posture). Sheriff's Office Council Liaison for three years; received Jacksonville Sheriff's endorsement in his 2022 special-election win; publicly states 'public safety is my top priority' — public_justice q0 TRUE. Public floor advocate for Ord. 2025-0147 (Jacksonville Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act, passed 12-5 in spring 2025), on the record: Jacksonville is 'standing with our president, with our governor, with our state legislature to secure our borders' — border_immigration q1/q2 TRUE. As Rules Committee Chair (May 2025), Howland was the first councilmember to publicly expose Jacksonville's covert City Hall gun registry (private security logging armed visitors' names and firearm types in apparent violation of FL §790.335 since July 2023); his disclosure led to a $5M state AG suit against the city and a DeSantis 'accountability' demand — self_defense q1 TRUE (opposes firearm registries; concrete affirmative-2A action). On Diamond's FY2025-26 abortion-defunding amendment, urged Diamond procedurally to withdraw and bring as standalone legislation (no substantive pro-life statement of his own) — sanctity_of_life left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage, family/schools (no city-level rubric-relevant vote), christian_liberty (no cited church or invocation statement), election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or self_defense q0 — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND.

profile.background: B.A. Duke (1995), M.B.A. Chicago Booth, U.S. Navy officer 1995-1999. Executive in defense, security, and marine-safety industries. Executive Director, The Fire Watch (Florida veteran-suicide-prevention nonprofit). First won At-Large Group 3 in Feb 2022 special election (succeeded late Tommy Hazouri); re-elected to full term to July 1, 2027. Council Vice President 2025-26; voted in 5/26/2026 as Council President for 2026-27 year (sworn 6/25/2026). Filed for 2027 AL-3 re-election; unopposed as of May 2026 with $320K raised.

candidates.office: City Council Member, District 12 (sitting · running for Duval County Tax Collector 2027)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 12, sitting, current term to July 1, 2027. Lame-duck on Council — launched April 15, 2025 campaign for Duval County Tax Collector (open seat, Overton-endorsed); raised ~$150K by June 2025. HARD evidence (cited): voted NO on Jacksonville's 2020 HRO LGBT-anti-discrimination expansion (Ord. 2020-0244), one of only 4 NO votes against 15 YES — biblical_marriage q4 TRUE (affirmatively opposed expansion of LGBT identity protections in city public policy). Voted AGAINST the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (10-9 passage), proposing instead to set aside $13.4M from the revenue drop specifically for the Sheriff's Office and Fire Rescue — fiscal stewardship q2 left null (he opposed the tax cut to keep revenue, mixed signal on deficit-restraint orientation). His $13.4M public-safety carve-out for JSO+JFRD is affirmative LE/first-responder advocacy beyond routine budget — public_justice q0 TRUE. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, schools/CRT/SOGI, invocation specifics, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A — those cells null. Notable separately: only two council members backed a 2022 public referendum on Confederate monuments; voted against renaming Confederate Street in 2024 (not directly rubric-mapped).

candidates.name: Rory Diamond
candidates.office: City Council Member, District 13 (Beaches), City of Jacksonville (sitting · term-limited, ends June 30, 2027 · widely reported as a likely 2027 mayoral candidate)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 13 council member (Beaches), term to June 30, 2027 (term-limited); widely reported as likely 2027 Jacksonville mayoral candidate. HARD evidence (cited): (a) sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE — publicly stated 'abortion to me and my family and to people who believe as I do is murder' when opposing reimbursement of city-employee abortion-travel; sponsored FY25-26 'Big Beautiful Budget Amendments' banning city funds for 'abortion and abortion-related services as defined by Florida law' — affirmative personhood-grade conviction statement. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) to RE-CODIFY Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting sexual orientation AND gender identity in employment, housing, and public accommodation (the same ordinance later used against Queen of Angels Catholic bookstore until a 2023 religious-exemption settlement). His 2025 anti-DEI budget amendments do not retroactively reverse the 2020 vote — q4 FALSE stands. (c) public_justice q0 TRUE — Jacksonville FOP endorsement in 2019; on the dais opposing 2020 defund amendments to JSO: 'To pretend that taking half the funding from our JSO is not defunding our police is just completely irrational... They're always one second away from death... I find this effort, this amendment, absolutely disgusting'; sustained K9s For Warriors veterans/LE-adjacent CEO bio (2014-2023); JAG officer bio — multiple independent affirmative-advocacy signals clearing the FXBG high bar. (d) border_immigration q2 TRUE — sponsored 2025 Jacksonville ordinance barring city grant recipients from using city money to support services for immigrants without permanent legal status; on the record: 'Jacksonville will never be a sanctuary city' and accused Mayor Deegan of 'making Jacksonville a sanctuary city under our noses' via her veto — direct anti-sanctuary, federal-preemption-aligned action. (e) self_defense q1 TRUE — publicly defended Florida's 2004 state firearms preemption statute against Jacksonville's de facto carry-permit registry (JSO carry logbooks): 'I absolutely support the law... it sure looks like it to me' (re: the AG's 2026 lawsuit) — direct opposition to a city-level gun registry under the q1 rubric scope (red-flag / mag limits / AWB / registries). NOT scored on sanctity_of_life q1 (abolition / personhood-grade legal regime): his policy posture operates within Florida's existing legal framework, not personhood/abolition. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite 1%-cap balanced-budget local legislation: q2 is narrowly about deficit spending / federal BBA, and the 1%-cap is local fiscal restraint — per the FXBG discipline that excludes 'generic low-tax claims' from q2, leave null. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach q2 despite anti-DEI budget amendments: anti-DEI cuts are local action against local programs, not refusal of state overreach. NOT scored on self_defense q0: Florida already has constitutional carry by state law (HB 543, 2023) — Diamond defended state preemption against city registries but no direct permitless-carry statement located. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage q0/q2, family/child-sovereignty, christian_liberty cells, election integrity, public_justice q1-q4 — null. Religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (specific church not located, though his pro-life quote 'people who believe as I do' implies religiously informed conviction).

profile.background: Rory Diamond — Jacksonville City Council District 13 (Atlantic Beach, Jacksonville Beach, Neptune Beach, Mayport, Intracoastal West). Elected May 2019; re-elected 2023 (defeated write-in only). Former litigator at international firms; former Special Assistant U.S. Attorney. Captain, U.S. Army / Florida National Guard JAG — deployed to the Middle East with the 40th Infantry Division in 2023 as Chief of Military Justice; returned to council duty Feb 2024. Former CEO (2014-2023) of K9s For Warriors (national veterans / service-dog nonprofit headquartered near Jacksonville); co-authored the federal PAWS Act (Puppies Assisting Wounded Servicemembers) signed in 2021 with then-Rep. Ron DeSantis. Wife reportedly Lauren Blocker. Term-limited from D13 after 2027; widely reported considering 2027 Jacksonville mayoral run.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Jacksonville At-Large Group 1 council member; Council President 2022-2023; second elected term; SUBMITTED RESIGNATION May 28, 2026, formally vacates Nov 2, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12 (HD-12 R primary Aug 18, 2026, currently unopposed; special election Aug 18, 2026). candidacy_status: running_higher_office. HARD evidence (cited): (a) biblical_marriage q2 FALSE + q4 FALSE — voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) on Ordinance 2020-0244 to RE-CODIFY Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting LGBT INCLUDING gender identity (after FL appellate court invalidated prior version on technical grounds); did NOT join the four-member dissent (White / Becton / Ferraro / Newby). Direct affirmative vote to codify gender-identity-as-protected-class (q2 FALSE) AND to advance LGBTQ+ identity in city public policy (q4 FALSE). No subsequent repeal effort during his Council Presidency. (b) public_justice q0 TRUE — campaign-site priority: 'Supporting Our First Responders to Fight Crime and Keep Jacksonville Safe'; Council Presidency 'ensured full funding' / 'record funding' for JSO and Fire & Rescue; Sheriff T.K. Waters HD-12 endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always backed the men and women in uniform'; FOP Lodge 5-30 (President Randy Reaves) endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always stood with the men and women who put on the badge'; X post defending JSO against 'Far-Left' attacks: 'T.K. Waters is an amazing leader who Jacksonville is lucky to have as our Sheriff' — multiple affirmative-advocacy signals + LE-leadership endorsements clearing the FXBG high bar. (c) border_immigration q2 TRUE — voted YES on Ordinance 2025-0138 (passed 6/12/25), prohibiting city tax dollars going to illegal immigrants and establishing city-level immigration enforcement coordination; on the record: 'This bill was not filed to hurt people... it was filed to ensure that government fulfills its top priority, which is to keep its citizens safe'; campaign-site claim 'helped lead the charge to prohibit tax dollars from going to illegal immigrants' — affirmative anti-sanctuary / federal-preemption-aligned action. NOT scored on sanctity_of_life q1: Freeman RECUSED from the FY25-26 budget vote (which included Diamond's pro-life/anti-DEI/anti-illegal-immigrant amendments) due to Miller Electric / city contract conflict — recusal is not a position; q1 null. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach: Freeman INVITED state DOGE oversight of city finances — the OPPOSITE of refusing state overreach; null. profile.background: Terrance Freeman — Jacksonville City Council At-Large Group 1, R; age ~51. Career: Executive Council Assistant to former Council Member Aaron Bowman starting 2015; subsequently Senior Project Manager / Senior Program Manager of Apprenticeship Programs at Miller Electric Company. Appointed by Gov. Rick Scott to Council District 10 in 2018; elected At-Large Group 1 in 2019; re-elected 2023 with 65% (100,297 votes — highest of any candidate on ballot). Council Vice President 2021; Council President 2022-2023. Chairs Rules Committee; serves on Finance Committee. Married, father of five. Bachelor's and master's degrees in physical education / sports psychology. Father was an Army veteran and Baptist minister; mother a career social worker. Submitted resignation May 28, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12; formally vacates council seat Nov 2, 2026; HD-12 Republican primary Aug 18, 2026 (currently unopposed); special election to fill council seat Aug 18, 2026.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Group 4, current term ends July 1, 2027 (TERM-LIMITED — Matt McCullough has entered race to replace him). Self-described moderate Republican; State Farm agent. HARD evidence (cited): publicly opposed Councilman Diamond's FY2025-26 budget amendment banning city funding for 'abortion and abortion-related services,' calling it a 'poison pill' of 'divisive rhetoric' and saying on the floor 'Have you no damn shame?' — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE. Co-sponsored Ord. 2020-0244 with Aaron Bowman and LeAnna Cumber restoring LGBT anti-discrimination protections to Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance (passed 15-4 in June 2020) — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (affirmatively expanded LGBT identity in city public policy). Voted NO on the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (10-9 passage), saying 'You can't cut your way to a greater city. I don't see waste' — local fiscal-restraint posture is opposed; left economic_stewardship q2 null (the question is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not millage votes). Has led council push to remove Confederate monuments (separate from rubric mapping). NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a cited church home, public statements on schools/CRT/SOGI/parental rights, invocation policy, election integrity, public-justice affirmative-advocacy, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A — those cells null.

profile.background: John Hugh 'Buddy' Dyer (b. Aug 7, 1958, Kissimmee FL). BA civil engineering Brown University; JD University of Florida College of Law. Civil engineer / attorney by training. FL State Senate 1992-2002 (Senate Minority Leader 1998-2000); 2002 Democratic nominee for FL Attorney General (lost to Charlie Crist). Mayor of Orlando since March 2003 — 48th and longest-serving mayor. Divorced Karen Caudill 2017 (2 sons); remarried Susie Pinson Galloway Shields 2021. 2005 absentee-ballot fraud charge dismissed; reinstated by Gov. Bush. Central public figure in 2016 Pulse nightclub aftermath.

profile.background: Antonio 'Tony' Ortiz. Orlando City Commissioner, District 2 (east Orlando / Azalea Park / Curry Ford / Lee Vista), since May 1, 2008; re-elected unopposed 2023; current term ends January 10, 2028. profile.background: Shaniqua 'Shan' Rose. Orlando City Commissioner, District 5, since June 24, 2024 (took office via special runoff after Gov. DeSantis suspended prior Commissioner Regina Hill in April 2024 over elder-abuse/fraud allegations); re-elected Nov 4, 2025 defeating Hill ~52%-33% with Lawanna Gelser ~15%; current 4-year term runs to 2029. Officially nonpartisan ballot; actual party affiliation Democrat (filed 2022 Democratic primary for FL House District 41; FL Dem Party chair Nikki Fried publicly celebrated her 2025 win; endorsed by Run For Something). Born to a St. Lucian immigrant family, raised in Brooklyn NY; relocated to Central Florida 2000. Education: Poinciana HS; AA General Studies, Valencia College (2007); BA Supervision & Management, Polk State College (2015); MA Public Administration, American Public University (2018). Career: former City of Orlando staff in Economic Development (Downtown Development Board/CRA) and Permitting Office; founder/CEO of Change For The Community (CFTC, est. July 4, 2019); Executive Director of Eatonville CRA. Civic: NAACP, League of Women Voters, American Red Cross, National Congress of Black Women. Single mother; widow (lost husband to gun violence).

profile.background: Dianne Williams-Cox (b. Quincy, FL). James A. Shanks HS honors graduate 1981; BS Data Processing Technology (FAMU); MBA (Nova Southeastern). Career: software developer GM; Harris Government Systems; 19 years State of Florida; FAMU adjunct professor. Currently Direct Services Quality Assurance Manager for Voices for Florida and owner of DWC Management Consulting Services. Married 33+ years to Thomas 'Tommy' Cox; three adult sons, six grandchildren. Founder, Southside United Citizens Action Alliance; former Tallahassee-Leon County Planning Commission; ran for FL House District 8 in 2014. 2018 Capital Outlook 'Community Activist of the Year.' First elected to Tallahassee City Commission Nov 2018; re-elected outright Aug 2022. Past Vice Chair Leon County DEC; past President Capital City Democratic Women's Club; Region 1 Chair Democratic Women of Florida. 40-year member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church Tallahassee, multiple ministries.

candidates.name: Curtis Richardson
candidates.office: City Commissioner, Seat 2, City of Tallahassee, Florida (first elected 2014; re-elected 2018; re-elected Nov 2024 in runoff defeating Dot Inman-Johnson; term to Nov 2028; currently serving as Mayor Pro Tem)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 2 City Commissioner and current Mayor Pro Tem (talgov.com bio + WFSU Nov 2024 re-election coverage + bethel1865.org board roster). Sweep of talgov.com, bethel1865.org, Tallahassee Reports, WCTV, WTXL, WFSU, Tallahassee Democrat, Panhandle Press, LiveAction, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 4 FALSE / 23 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: christian_liberty.0 (Bethel AME Church Tallahassee usher AND trustee — explicit public Christian profession on both his city bio and the church's official board roster). public_justice.0 (sustained affirmative pro-LE advocacy clears the high-bar: led FY2024 millage hike specifically to fund 20 ADDITIONAL TPD officers and TPD technology; voted YES on $625K federal grant to hire police officers Nov 2025; publicly defended pro-police budget at conservative candidate forum — 'We raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' — not just a budget vote, an explicit pro-LE program). border_immigration.2 (Oct 22, 2025 voted AGAINST motion to rescind Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement with ICE — agreement REMAINS in force; TPD cooperates with ICE; vote stands even though rhetoric framed reluctant compliance with state-removal threats). FALSE: sanctity_of_life.0 (explicit pro-choice on record: 'I believe in a woman's right to choose' and 'that is a choice between my wife and me'; 2021 NO vote on the city's pro-choice resolution was procedural disbelief that the Legislature would listen, NOT a pro-life vote — LiveAction + WCTV). family_child_sovereignty.3 (self-identified as 'one of the biggest opponents' of Jeb Bush's opportunity scholarship program — opposes FL school choice / voucher expansion). economic_stewardship.2 (voted YES on FY2025 budget 3-2 at 4.42 mills above rollback AND on FY2026 budget at 4.4072 mills; explicitly rejected the rollback proposal that would have required ~$3.9M of cuts — 'we raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' is honest framing, but it scores FALSE on the fiscal-restraint cell as written). refuse_state_overreach.0 (re: 287(g) said 'It would not be in my best interest to put myself in a position to potentially be removed from office' — explicit compliance posture on a conservative-rubric-coded state mandate). NULL HOLDS: biblical_marriage NULL — Tallahassee historically issued Pride proclamations under his tenure but no specific Richardson floor vote was sourced this batch; the 2026 unanimous review-of-DEI vote was compliance with state law, not affirmative marriage defense; honest hold. self_defense NULL — no sourced 2A statement post-FSU April 2025 shooting located. All other cells NULL — no cited evidence on sanctity_of_life q1-4, biblical_marriage q0-4, family q0-2,4, christian_liberty q1-4, economic_stewardship q0/1/3/4, election_integrity (any), public_justice q1-4, refuse_state_overreach q1-4, border_immigration q0/1/3/4, self_defense (any).

profile.background: Curtis B. Richardson (b. Oct 8, 1956, Jacksonville, FL; raised Green Cove Springs). BS Psychology (FSU); MA Counseling Psychology (UWF); MS School Psychology (FSU 1983). School psychologist Gadsden County 1983-1991; staff to FL Education Commissioner Betty Castor; Deputy Director of Cabinet Affairs under Gov. Lawton Chiles; Director of School Improvement Gadsden. Leon County School Board 1990-1996 (first Black male elected, served as Chair). Florida House of Representatives District 8 (D, Tallahassee) 4 terms 2000-2008. Tallahassee City Commission Seat 2 since 2014 (re-elected 2018, re-elected Nov 2024 over Dot Inman-Johnson). Currently Mayor Pro Tem. Married to Leon County Judge Nina Ashenafi-Richardson. Long-time member of Bethel AME Tallahassee (usher / trustee).

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Seat 3, sworn Nov 19, 2018; term ends Nov 16, 2026; NOT seeking re-election to Seat 3 — filed Aug 11, 2025 to run for Tallahassee Mayor in the Aug 18, 2026 primary (Seat 3 will be open). candidates.name: Kenneth T. Welch
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 54th Mayor of St. Petersburg, sworn Jan 6, 2022; declared candidate in Aug 18, 2026 primary (Wikipedia 2026 mayoral race page). March 2026 polling showed Welch (19%) trailing Charlie Crist (23%) in a 7-way race incl. Brandi Gabbard, Jim Large (R), Maria Scruggs, Kevin Batdorf, Paul Congemi. HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: Welch openly identifies with his family's COGIC heritage and publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his guiding principles (prayer first) — christian_liberty q0 TRUE (Tampa Magazines long-form interview). HARD evidence on LGBTQ+ cells: personally raises the Pride flag over City Hall every year (12th annual 2025); 2022 proclamation: 'We cannot and will not stand silent while our fundamental rights of freedom, choice and self-determination are stripped by anyone … including politicians in Washington and Tallahassee'; 2026 wrapped City Hall steps in Pride colors and pledged ~$300K-equivalent in-kind public-safety support to St. Pete Pride despite SB 1134 (St. Pete Catalyst, Florida Politics, CL Tampa) — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. AFFIRMATIVE policing posture: $171M SPPD budget, body cams/tasers/drones, public partnership with Chief Holloway, publicly thanked Rep. Luna for $480K in federal cruiser funding ('This kind of support means a lot … setting a national standard') — public_justice q0 TRUE (St. Pete Catalyst, St Pete Rising). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: maintains SPPD's 287(g) ICE partnership and has REFUSED activist pressure to cancel it ('Mayor Ken Welch has the capacity to get rid of the 287(g) agreement, but he is actively choosing not to do so' — Florida Phoenix / WMNF); St. Pete is NOT a sanctuary city — border_immigration q2 TRUE. ECON: FY23 budget approved with ~13% effective property-tax increase (~$20.5M more revenue, St. Pete Catalyst); Welch publicly blasted Crist for proposing tax cuts — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE. ABORTION: administration raised 'process and precedent' concerns about the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund grant proposal (council killed 6-2); no affirmative pro-life position either — sanctity_of_life cells null. STATE PUSHBACK: led FL mayors against SB 1134 anti-DEI bill and defied it on Pride; this is local-control posture but directionally leftward (defending DEI/Pride), so refuse_state_overreach cells null under the conservative-rubric reading. NO direct evidence on schools (mayor lane), election administration, christian_liberty q2-q4, public_justice q1-q4, or self-defense (FL preemption blocks municipal gun regs) — those cells null.

profile.religion: Pentecostal Church of God in Christ (COGIC) family heritage — uncle Clarence pastored Prayer Tower COGIC in the Gas Plant District; father David sang in the choir and taught Sunday school there. Publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his four guiding principles (prayer listed first).
profile.education: MBA, Florida A&M University; BA Accounting, University of South Florida
profile.background: 54th Mayor of St. Petersburg, first elected Nov 2021 (St. Pete's first Black mayor), sworn Jan 6, 2022; running for re-election in the Aug 18, 2026 primary (Nov 3, 2026 runoff if needed). Prior: Pinellas County Commissioner 2000-2022 (District 7). Accountant at Florida Power Corporation. Third-generation St. Pete native (b. Aug 15, 1964). Wife Donna, two daughters.

candidates.name: Copley Gerdes
candidates.notes: St Petersburg City Council D1 since Jan 6, 2022; re-elected UNOPPOSED Nov 5, 2024; current term to Jan 4, 2029; Council CHAIR since Dec 2024 (also Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair). Officially NP ballot. [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] HARD evidence: (a) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — as Council Chair, PERSONALLY INTRODUCED the June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising ceremony at City Hall (Tampa Bay Times / Watermark Out News); in October 2025 voted YES to advance Councilmember Driscoll's 'Harmony' (LGBTQ+) and 'History' (Black history) city-flag variations (joining Driscoll, Givens, Figgs-Sanders, Floyd, Hanewicz; only Harting voted no). His own quote: 'I want to throw a haymaker… I don't want to throw a jab.' (b) economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — as Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair voted YES on the FY24 city budget that Catalyst headlined as 'begrudgingly approves budget, 13% tax increase'; YES on the $287.5M Rays stadium bonds + $142M Gas Plant District bonds (4-3, July 2024); YES on the original 5-3 stadium agreement. Pattern: chairs the budget committee, votes for major debt issuance + tax increases. NULL on sanctity_of_life (no St Pete pro-life resolution; no Gerdes statement on abortion), family_child_sovereignty (Pinellas school board is separate authority), christian_liberty (Catholic faith documented in profile.religion but no policy vote — religion is biographical not scoreable on its own per evidence-first rule), election_integrity (no Gerdes-specific election-admin vote), public_justice (CALL — Community Assistance & Life Liaison — is a partial-diversion social-worker-first model that diverts certain 911 calls FROM sworn officers; Gerdes praised CALL participation; no affirmative pro-police statement clears the rubric's high bar), refuse_state_overreach (his pro-LGBTQ-flag stance opposing DeSantis state actions defends progressive priorities — not the rubric's conservative-subsidiarity anchor; held null), border_immigration (the 2017 'sanctuary city' comments were from his FATHER Charlie Gerdes, NOT Copley — do not attribute), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts). DISCIPLINE FLAGS: father/son distinction matters (Charlie Gerdes was the 2017 sanctuary-city commenter); Catholic-school + Catholic-university religion degree is biographically suggestive but NOT scored as a policy verdict per evidence-first rule.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (St. Petersburg Catholic High School alumnus; B.A. in Religion with Catholic Studies emphasis from Saint Leo University, a Catholic institution; personal faith is documented but no specific policy position derived from it)
profile.background: Charles 'Copley' Gerdes. City of St. Petersburg, Florida City Council District 1 since January 6, 2022 (special election to replace Robert Blackmon); re-elected UNOPPOSED Nov 5, 2024; current term ends January 4, 2029. Officially NONPARTISAN ballot. Council CHAIR since December 2024 (elected after five rounds of voting); also Chair of Budget, Finance & Taxation Committee. Listed #21 on Florida Politics' Tampa Bay Most Powerful Politicians. Family: son of former St. Pete Councilmember Charlie Gerdes (also held D1) and nephew of city administrator Rob Gerdes. Education: St. Petersburg Catholic HS; B.A. Religion (Catholic Studies emphasis), Saint Leo University. Profession: financial advisor with Northwestern Mutual since 2011; also Director of Special Projects / Strategic Development at family law firm Keane, Reese, Vesely & Gerdes P.A. Married Cecily Valenty (2016); two children. Catholic faith publicly documented via education path but no derived policy position scored.

candidates.name: Brandi Gabbard
candidates.notes: St Petersburg City Council D2 (NP ballot, personal D registration); term to Jan 7, 2027 — TERM-LIMITED; filed March 2026 for 2026 St Pete Mayor race vs incumbent Welch (candidacy_status running_higher_office). [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] HARD evidence: (a) sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE — Feb 23, 2023 Council Chair Gabbard initially SUPPORTED a $50,000 city allocation to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund; on April 6, 2023 reversed and voted against it but explicitly cited political-risk calculus (state-Republican retaliation threats, Disney/Andrew Warren examples), NOT pro-life conviction. Initial pro-fund support is the dispositive position. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — attended/participated in June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising at City Hall during 'Shine with Pride 2026'; posted official 'Happy Pride St Pete' on D2 Facebook; as mayoral candidate (April 2026) condemned FL anti-DEI law SB 1134 ('every resident — regardless of their race or whom they love — has an equal seat at the table'). (c) economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — voted YES on the $6.5B Rays Stadium / Gas Plant District deal July 18, 2024 (5-3) AND on the Dec 5, 2024 bond authorization (long-term city debt for a private-team stadium). Quote: 'We need to move this forward because, quite frankly, I think we're calling their bluff by doing so.' Also supports general-obligation infrastructure bonds (future-generation debt justified by resilience needs). NULL on family/school (Pinellas school board is separate authority), christian_liberty (no public stance; religion not stated), election_integrity (Pinellas SoE handles elections), public_justice (no affirmative pro-police statement located; mayoral 'public safety' platform pillar lacks specifics), refuse_state_overreach (her anti-DEI/anti-preemption stance defends progressive priorities — not the rubric's conservative-subsidiarity anchor; held null), border_immigration (no Gabbard-specific ICE/sanctuary statement located; she joined council Jan 2018 after the 2017 sanctuary-city statement under Mayor Kriseman), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts).

profile.background: Brandi Gabbard. City of St. Petersburg, Florida City Council District 2 (NE St. Pete — Barcley Estates / Northeast) since January 2018; re-elected unopposed 2021; current term ends January 7, 2027 (TERM-LIMITED). Officially NONPARTISAN ballot; personal voter registration Democrat. Filed March 2026 to run for St. Petersburg MAYOR in November 2026, challenging incumbent Mayor Ken Welch — positioning to the LEFT of Welch on DEI/LGBTQ issues. Past Council Chair. Indiana native; moved to St. Petersburg 2003 with husband Keith; one son in Barcley Estates. Real-estate broker-associate at Smith & Associates (also Suncoast Realty Solutions); 18+ years in Tampa Bay, $70M+ in sales. 2014 President, Pinellas Realtor Organization; 2015 Pinellas Realtor of the Year; 2018 Chair, NAR Insurance Committee (testified before FL Governor and Cabinet on flood insurance). Religion / church affiliation not publicly stated.

candidates.name: Mike Harting
candidates.office: City Council, District 3, City of St. Petersburg, Florida (elected Nov 5, 2024 runoff over Pete Boland with 50.38%; sworn in Jan 2, 2025; term to Jan 4, 2029; succeeded term-limited Republican Ed Montanari)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 3 Councilmember (stpete.org D3 bio + Florida Politics Nov 2024 runoff coverage + Bay News 9 swearing-in). Sweep of stpete.org, mike4stpete.com, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia, Bay News 9, WUSF, St. Pete Catalyst, Tampa Bay Business & Wealth, San Pedro Gazette. HARD evidence — 1 TRUE / 1 FALSE / 28 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: biblical_marriage.4 — Oct 9 2025 Harting was the LONE NO vote on Driscoll's Harmony (Pride colors) + History flag resolution, arguing the existing city flag should represent all 280,000 St. Pete residents equally and that 'inclusive' was being misapplied to elevate two specific subgroups; the resolution was withdrawn before a final binding roll-call but his on-the-record opposition is documented in both WUSF and St. Pete Catalyst coverage — clears TRUE per the rubric. FALSE: economic_stewardship.2 — voted AYE on the FY2025-26 budget at 6.4525 millage (3.93% above rollback) without on-record restraint advocacy at adoption; only Driscoll + Givens dissented; his Dec 2025 'budget stress test' request post-dated the adoption and was reactive to anticipated state property-tax cuts (St. Pete Catalyst). NULL HOLDS: christian_liberty.0 NULL — no public Christian profession in bio, campaign site, or press coverage; absence of profession is NULL, NOT FALSE. public_justice.0 NULL — no affirmative sustained pro-LE program in first ~18 months in seat; honest hold. border_immigration NULL — no on-record statement on SPPD 287(g) located despite Feb 2026 public-comment campaign. religion NULL — registered without party affiliation, no faith profession found. All other 26 cells NULL after sweep — no statements on life, marriage q0-3, family/schools, religious liberty, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, broader border, or 2A located.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND
profile.background: Mike Harting — founder/CEO of 3 Daughters Brewing (named for daughters Meadow, Livvi, Madi with wife Leigh). University of Florida finance graduate; former Outback Steakhouse JVP; co-owner of Bella Brava; USF adjunct professor. Father retired as Lt. Col. (1st Cavalry, Vietnam vet); family moved to St. Pete via MacDill AFB in the 1980s. Registered without party affiliation; endorsement coalition spanned R (former Mayor Rick Baker, ex-Sen. Jeff Brandes, Blackmon) and D (Sen. Darryl Rouson, Councilmember Driscoll). Narrowly defeated Pete Boland Nov 5 2024 runoff to replace term-limited Republican Ed Montanari; sworn in Jan 2, 2025.

candidates.name: Esteban Bovo Jr.
candidates.office: Mayor of Hialeah, Florida (former · resigned April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group, a D.C. lobbying firm)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] CURRENCY FIX: Bovo is NOT the sitting Mayor of Hialeah — he resigned April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group, a D.C. lobbying firm. Status moved to former; candidacy_status not_running. Interim mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves now holds the seat. HARD evidence (cited) on retained positions: (a) biblical_marriage q2 TRUE — as Miami-Dade Commissioner Dec 2, 2014, Bovo voted NO on the transgender-inclusive Human Rights Ordinance (passed 8-3), publicly saying 'There are cases of the deviants who will take advantage of this' and conditioning support on a bathroom-use carve-out; direct rejection of gender-identity-as-protected-class framing. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — Bovo CO-SPONSORED the Miami-Dade LGBTQ Advisory Board ordinance in 2019, publicly thanked by Equality Florida; that is direct affirmative promotion of LGBTQ+ public policy. The record is genuinely mixed (q2 anti / q4 pro) and is scored that way per the no-overread, no-inference rule. (c) public_justice q0 TRUE — 2020 mayoral campaign explicitly opposed defunding/restructuring police: 'Rethinking everything about our police only feeds into what I think is a very, very loud, minority'; police-union support based on no-defund record; affirmative anti-defund advocacy clearing the FXBG high bar. (d) border_immigration q2 TRUE — as Hialeah Mayor pushed Resolution 2025-073 (March 2025) authorizing 287(g) MOA with ICE, explicitly framed as avoiding sanctuary-city designation under Tallahassee pressure. NOT scored on border_immigration q1 (mandatory deportation): Bovo explicitly HEDGED — 'If the question is, are we gonna pull people and ask for their immigration papers? That's not what we're trained to do, that's not what we're authorized to do' — runs against the q1 high bar. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach despite 2020-21 anti-mask-mandate statements ('I'm not in favor of mandates on anything'): he was ALIGNED with DeSantis, not refusing state directives — DeSantis was also anti-mandate, so this isn't 'local resistance to state overreach' on the rubric reading. NOT scored on christian_liberty q3 despite routine Hialeah council invocation practice: invocations are institutional, not Bovo-led action. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (the Hialeah pro-life-clinic-vandalism 'hate crime' framing is sympathetic but case-specific, not a personhood/abolition statement), school-choice / parental rights, election integrity, or self-defense — those cells null. DEVIATION ALERT: R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q4 (2019 LGBTQ Advisory Board co-sponsorship).

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (parishioner; specific parish not extractable from the official Hialeah bio PDF — binary not OCR-readable in this sweep)
profile.education: Miami Dade College (AA), Florida International University (BA)
profile.background: Esteban 'Steve' Bovo Jr., b. June 12, 1962 in Queens, NY to Esteban Bovo Carás (2506 Brigade / Bay of Pigs veteran). AA Business Administration, Miami-Dade Community College (1983); BS Political Science, Florida International University (1987). Pre-politics: marketing/advertising executive, Hialeah Park Race Track. Career: Hialeah City Council 1998-2008; Florida House District 110 (2008-2011, resigned to run for county); Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 13 (2011-2020, served as Chair); ran for Miami-Dade Mayor 2020 (won R primary, lost general); elected Mayor of Hialeah Nov 2, 2021; RESIGNED April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group lobbying firm in D.C., following wife Viviana Bovo (longtime Marco Rubio staffer, now State Department). Five children: Oscar, Bianca, Esteban, Sofia, Alessandro. Interim Mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves (City Council President) sworn in April 2025 — Hialeah's first female mayor; special election Nov 2025.

Announced resignation May 1, 2026 effective Nov 2, 2026; withdrawing 2028 re-election candidacy; special election Nov 3, 2026 to fill the remainder of the term. candidates.notes: HIGH-DEVIATION-RICH archetype-5 'Constitutional Sheriff' profile — Trump-aligned + 2A maximalist + anti-sanctuary + anti-mandate + openly Christian framing. 14 TRUE cells across PJ/RSO/BI/SD/CL/FCS clusters. HEADLINE TRUE CLUSTER: (1) RSO 5/5 TRUE — 2013 signed statement refusing federal gun-control enforcement, 'we are not the mask police' Oct 2021 deputies-will-not-enforce-school-masks, 'stands with America's workforce in fight against vaccine mandates' Oct 2021, rebuked DOJ for 'threats of violence' framing of school-board-protest parents, 'Constitutional Sheriff' public identity; (2) BI 2/2 TRUE — 'You bring that crap to Brevard County, and you're going to end up in jail' anti-sanctuary, Brevard launched 287(g) in 2019 (pre-DeSantis statewide), Feb 2025 joined DeSantis Illegal Immigration Street Task Force, BCSO awarded ~$700K immigration-enforcement funding Feb 2026; (3) SD 2/2 TRUE — viral 'arm up and prepare for an attack' video calling on citizens to arm themselves, attended April 2023 constitutional carry signing 'Criminals don't go get a permit,' Sept 2025 open-carry message announcing deputies will not enforce ban after 1st DCA ruling; (4) PJ 4/5 TRUE — Wheel of Fugitive + chain-gang program (modeled on Arpaio 2013) + 'encouraging citizens to arm themselves and confront bad guys before deputies arrive' (Marshall Project), 'zero tolerance for crime keeps FL safe' flvoicenews, June 2025 anti-riot 'we will kill you, graveyard dead' press conference with AG Uthmeier, right-wing-law-enforcement-ideology alignment; (5) CL 3/4 TRUE — Defended 'In God We Trust' patrol-car decals on Fox & Friends Nov 2019, rebuked DOJ school-board-parents-as-threats memo, spoke at Trinity Wellsprings Church Men's Ministry Breakfast March 2020 'about his faith and protecting the foundation of the nation'; (6) FCS q0 TRUE — 'We are not the mask police' framed around parents' authority over their children. SINGLE FALSE DEVIATION: PJ q4 — Gregory Edwards 2018 in-custody death (Army combat vet, restraint chair, pepper spray, spit hood, stun-gun barbs in back); BCSO fought 2+ years to release jail video, FLORIDA TODAY had to sue under Public Records Act (released Nov 2020); federal §1983 force claims dismissed Mar 2023 but state negligence claim survived — pattern of accountability resistance, not reform. SOL all NULL — sheriff lane discipline; no abortion statement located; do NOT infer from R-party or evangelical framing. BM all NULL — no specific marriage/SOGI/Pride statement located. EC q2 NULL — no documented fiscal-restraint posture in agency. CAREFUL DATA HYGIENE: research agent honestly flagged that prior batch-prompt confused (a) Marion County's Susan Lorincz/Santiago-Miranda case (Sheriff Billy Woods, NOT Brevard), (b) GA Laken Riley case (Jose Ibarra) with the Brevard Aug 2021 Paris Wilder traffic-stop ambush (deputy Brian Potters wounded NOT killed) — neither belongs to Ivey's record; not scored.

candidates.name: Paul Alfrey
candidates.office: Mayor of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · 2nd term, ends Nov 2028; current charter is 2 consecutive 4-year terms)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Mayor of Melbourne, FL (2nd term to Nov 2028); not on 2026 ballot. Party R (Republican Mayors Association affiliate publicly recognized 2024 re-election). HARD evidence (cited): In Sept 2022 publicly defended the Space Coast Pride permit — which included a drag queen story time component — against State Rep. Randy Fine's demand the city pull the permit, posting on Facebook that drag queen story time 'is an event that happens nationwide and is attended by both adults and children' and that the organizers 'had as much right to organize as any.' This is affirmative defense of city-permitted LGBT promotion in public policy against a same-party state-rep challenge — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE. In Feb 2022 personally led Melbourne's 'Just Say No' anti-panhandling public-safety campaign rolled out as a Mayor+MPD partnership ('Just say no' message + city plan); 15-year retired Melbourne PD officer (K-9, Street Crimes, Community Policing, FTO) whose LE identity is centerpiece of his official bio; in June 2020 publicly defended the office of LE while distancing from a single FOP-president-personal-speech incident ('whoever put this up... they need to go') — affirmative pro-LE public-safety advocacy under the same standard applied to Vice Mayor Neuman's Jan 2025 MPD-partnership homelessness resolution → public_justice q0 TRUE. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite his '10-year low millage' boast: the question is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not generic low-tax claims. In Sept 2025 personally proposed renaming a stretch of Cypress Avenue to 'Charlie Kirk Lane' after Kirk's assassination ('He will not be forgotten in Melbourne!') and promoted 12+ Brevard County memorial billboards; council voted 5-1 against (only Vice Mayor Neuman supported). This is ideological signal but does not cleanly map to a christian_liberty rubric cell (Kirk Lane is a memorial naming of a Christian-affiliated public figure, not a Christian symbol or prayer policy) → christian_liberty cells null per the no-overread rule. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, transgender ideology, family/parental rights/schools (city does not run schools), invocation/Nativity policy, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, sanctuary/ICE cooperation, or 2A — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. DEVIATION ALERT (R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q4).
