candidates.name: Thad Altman
candidates.notes: MIXED MODERATE-R PROFILE — 30+ year FL legislator with substantial pro-life + parental-rights + E-Verify signature WITH meaningful red-flag-gun-control + universal-voucher-skeptic deviations. HARD-TRUE CLUSTER (5): (1) sanctity_of_life q0+q1 — HB 5 2022 15-week abortion ban YES + HB 1429 2018 dismemberment-abortion ban YES (consistent pro-life voting); (2) biblical_marriage q2+q4 + family q0+q2 — HB 1557 2022 Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay') YES (signature parental-rights + biological-sex + SOGI-curriculum-opposition vote); (3) border q2 — HB 89 2018 E-Verify LEAD SPONSOR + anti-sanctuary HB 9 cosponsor (strong border/sanctuary-opposition signature). HARD-FALSE CLUSTER (2 significant deviations): (1) family q1 (school choice) — voted NO on HB 1 2023 universal-voucher expansion per Freedom Index, breaking with DeSantis-era FL GOP consensus on universal school choice (notable break for a long-tenured R); (2) self_defense q0+q1 — voted YES on SB 7026 2018 Parkland Marjory Stoneman Douglas Public Safety Act (raised long-gun age to 21, created red-flag/risk-protection orders, 3-day waiting period) + publicly DEFENDED his support including the red-flag provisions in 2023 Phoenix retrospective. ECON q2 FALSE — career Kennedy Space Center pork-advocacy is his signature legislative legacy (his Wikipedia identifies space-industry funding as defining issue), not budget-restraint. PUBLIC JUSTICE all NULL — no direct 'publicly backed police' affirmative statement located meeting the LOCAL-skill HIGH-BAR requirement (voting a budget that funds police is NOT enough); HB 1 2021 anti-riot individual vote not located. CHRISTIAN LIBERTY all NULL — no specific religious-liberty votes located. ELECTION INTEGRITY q1 NULL — SB 90 2021 and SB 524 2022 specific votes not located individually despite being in House. REFUSE_STATE q0-q4 ALL NULL — only ~5 months in county office; no Brevard BoCC RSO-relevant vote located (Bill of Rights Sanctuary County December 2024 ordinance was pulled by Feltner before vote per Yahoo News; Brevard 2025 millage vote not pulled). State-leg HB 1B Nov 2021 anti-vaccine-mandate Special Session YES vote is REFUSE-FEDERAL not REFUSE-STATE territory — kept honest-NULL on RSO. Catholic religion per Vote Smart bio; Endorsed Terry Cronin March 2025 as 'Trump Republican.' Estimated net /100: ~28-35 — moderate-R archetype with pro-life + HB 1557 + E-Verify TRUE cluster offset by red-flag + universal-voucher-NO + space-pork FALSE deviations.

candidates.notes: ARCHETYPE: admin-only constitutional officer WITH measurable election-integrity record — third FL Brevard constitutional-officer pattern alongside (a) pure roster_verified [Blickley Property Appraiser + Cullen Tax Collector] and (b) evidence-local-with-fiscal-watchdog-audit [Sadoff Clerk/Comptroller]. Unlike Blickley/Cullen pure-admin, Bobanic earns evidence_local because his statutory wheelhouse IS election integrity — producing measurable output on the rubric's election-integrity axis. THREE TRUE cells: (1) election_integrity q0 — implemented and operates Brevard's 100% Clear Ballot independent post-election audit covering every race + precinct + ballot with 99.992% certified-to-audit match in 2024 General; all FL votes on paper, 22-month retention per statute; (2) election_integrity q1 — office enforces FL photo-ID-with-signature requirement at Early Voting + Election Day, mail ballots require name + DOB + addresses + DL or last-4-SSN + signature match; implemented SigView signature-verification system, won 2023 EAC Clearinghouse Award; (3) public_justice q1 — when Burns v. Bobanic mandamus alleged unequal treatment of R vs D candidate qualifying paperwork, Bobanic responded with internal audit + fired responsible staffer + publicly noted 3 of 4 disqualified candidates that cycle were Republicans (suggests non-partisan administration); Circuit Judge Scott Blaue dismissed suit for lack of standing. ALL OTHER RUBRIC CELLS NULL per LOCAL skill admin-only-constitutional-officer protocol — no findable statements on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family/child sovereignty, christian_liberty, border/immigration, self-defense, or broader fiscal positions outside statutory office duties. Religion not findable. EI q4 NULL — FL statutorily banned private election grants in SB 90 (2021); Brevard operates under ban but no Bobanic-specific public Zuckerbucks/CTCL statement located.

candidates.name: Rachel Sadoff
candidates.office: Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller, Brevard County
candidates.notes: ARCHETYPE: career-administrator R-comptroller continuing Scott-Ellis-watchdog tradition; SINGLE HARD-TRUE on economic_stewardship q2 for the 2022 Lober purchasing-card audit. Headline finding: After unanimous BoCC request February 22 2022, Sadoff conducted full audit of Commissioner Bryan Lober (R, then-District 2)'s ~$40K p-card spending. Final audit concluded card use 'was often indiscriminate, wasteful, and not for a legitimate public use.' Over $9,000 unaccounted-for items; suspected misuse of tax-exemption certificate; personal email used to evade public-records law. Referred to FDLE for criminal investigation November 2022. When Lober personally requested she NOT audit him, Sadoff publicly disclosed the request: 'I would never let a friendship influence how myself or my office exercises authority under the law' (Space Coast Rocket, 2022). She also rejected suggestion that contracted auditors are preferable to an elected comptroller answerable to voters. Sadoff v. Board of County Commissioners of Brevard County (2022) appellate-court litigation exists per FindLaw — confirming her willingness to take BoCC to court as Comptroller. Stated mission to 'expand upon Clerk Ellis's legacy of transparency, accountability, and accessibility' — Ellis was the famously combative gadfly; Sadoff has delivered. CONSTITUTIONAL-OFFICE ARCHETYPE BREAK: unlike the other 3 Brevard constitutional officers in this batch (Blickley/Cullen/Ivey-prior who are roster_verified-only), Sadoff has produced a named-target, named-amount, public-record fiscal-watchdog action against a fellow Republican commissioner — a real on-record-deviation TRUE on economic_stewardship q2. Biblical marriage q0 honest-NULL: was deputy clerk (Viera branch manager) in 2015 when Obergefell came down, NOT a policymaker; Brevard's then-Clerk Scott Ellis quietly complied (no public refusal incident comparable to Baker Co Stacie Harvey or KY Kim Davis); no Sadoff public statement on Obergefell, same-sex marriage policy, or conscience-refusal located. Catholic-affiliation is proxy-strength (St Joseph Catholic School board + Barry MPA at Adrian-Dominican Catholic institution), not self-declared. Other categories NULL per office-scope + no findable positions.

profile.religion: Catholic (proxy: Saint Joseph Catholic School board service + Barry University MPA — not self-declared)
profile.background: Brevard County Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller since January 1 2021 (sworn ad interim Jan 1 2021 after predecessor Scott Ellis resigned Dec 31 2020; officially sworn Jan 5 2021). First female Brevard Clerk in 167 years. Dual-hat role: Clerk + County Comptroller + Auditor + County Recorder. Career deputy clerk: started Brevard Clerk's office 1997 under Sandy Crawford; rose to Viera Branch Manager (Viera + Brevard County Jail). First elected 2020 unopposed after Sandra Ruizzo failed to qualify in GOP primary. Re-elected unopposed 2024 through December 31 2028. EFSC (B.A. Professional Studies); Barry University (MPA). Married to Steve Sadoff; three children. Board service: Saint Joseph Catholic School (Palm Bay), Space Coast Marine Institute youth diversion program, Bays Florida. Official bio language: family 'values public service, veterans, and law enforcement.'

profile.background: Lisa Cullen, CFC, is the elected constitutional officer Tax Collector of Brevard County, Florida (Republican). Born in Logan, West Virginia; family relocated to Brevard County early 1980s. Education: Merritt Island High School; A.A. Brevard Community College / Eastern Florida State College; B.S. Accounting & Finance, University of Central Florida. Career: began at the Brevard Tax Collector's Office front counter in 1985; Certified Florida Collector (CFC) since February 2009. First elected November 2008; assumed office January 2009; re-elected unopposed in 2012, 2016, and 2020. August 20, 2024 Republican primary: defeated County Commissioner Rita Pritchett 84.8% to 15.9%; write-in opponent Daniel Edwin Lopez withdrew early September 2024 making her re-elected unopposed in the general for a term to January 2029. President of the Florida Tax Collectors Association 2014-2015. Married to high-school sweetheart Robert Cullen, a RETIRED Brevard County Sheriff's Office Lieutenant. EFSC Foundation Board (2012-2018, Board Chair); Michael-Gene Basketball Foundation; National Homeless Veterans Support. 2016 TDC Portrait of Excellence; 2022 EFSC Distinguished Alumni. Rated 'A' by Ultra MAGA Patriots Brevard County (May 2024).

profile.religion: No public faith profession located
profile.background: Mike Jaffe — 20+ year Palm Bay resident. Director of Operations for a regional home builder; small-business owner; licensed FL realtor. Married 22 years to high-school sweetheart Kim; two children (ages 9 and 7). Little League coach. Self-described fiscal conservative focused on infrastructure (flooding / Indian River Lagoon), public safety budget protection, and responsible development. Reports knocking 5,000+ doors during 2024 campaign. Won Seat 5 with 44.24% on Nov 5, 2024 (5-way special election); sworn in + unanimously elevated to Deputy Mayor Nov 20, 2024 (Hammer nominated). Authored the Oct 17, 2025 motion to censure colleague Chandler Langevin (3-2) over Langevin's 'Deport every Indian immediately' remarks and citation of Robert Rundo, requesting Gov. DeSantis suspend Langevin under FS 112.51 + an ethics complaint. Soft-R signal: spoke at the Brevard Republican Party Straw Poll (June 22, 2024); no primary-source party registration confirmed. Announced re-election bid July 1, 2025.

profile.religion: Christian — publicly self-identified: 'committed to aligning with individuals who dedicate their talents and skills to advance God's purpose and fulfill the Great Commission'; mission 'to bring unity within the body of Christ, fostering connections that are grounded in love, service, and faith.' Fellowship of Christian Athletes 2005-2012; has spoken at Greater Palm Bay Church of God events. Denomination not specified.
profile.background: Kenny Johnson, MPA — Melbourne, FL native; Bayside High School; full football scholarship to Charleston Southern University (defensive back); B.S. Criminal Justice; Master of Public Administration. Member of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity. Returned to Brevard 2013; juvenile case manager, juvenile detention officer, teacher, coach, personal trainer. Founded Top Notch Training of Brevard, Inc. (2016) and the 'Top Notch Leaders' mentoring program for 5th-6th-grade boys. First elected to Palm Bay Council Seat 4 in 2018 (~58%); re-elected 2022. Currently Deputy Mayor (rotating peer designation, sequence overlaps with Jaffe) + Chair of the Florida League of Cities Finance & Taxation Policy Committee. Established Palm Bay's Annual Black History Event, Inaugural Veterans Day Parade, Youth Advisory Board, and Civil Citation Program for misdemeanor cannabis (diversion alternative to arrest). Father; local business owner. Notably feuded with then-State Rep Randy Fine (2022 confrontation; State Attorney declined to charge Johnson — 'no prosecutable crimes').

profile.background: Chandler Langevin — Brevard County native, Titusville High grad. ~10 years U.S. Navy Hospital Corpsman (FMF) attached to USMC — NATO Liaison with 4th ANGLICO in Afghanistan; sniper-platoon medic with 1st Bn 25th Marines in the Pacific. Took early honorable discharge over the Biden COVID vaccine mandate ('unwillingness to be a guinea pig'). Works in veteran advocacy; founded local youth program 'Florida Scouts'; State Director, American Restoration Coalition. Previously on Odyssey Charter School board (removed Oct 2025 over the censure controversy). Briefly filed for FL HD-33 in 2023 before withdrawing and endorsing Monique Miller. Active with Republican Liberty Caucus (Brevard chapter), Moms for Liberty (Brevard), and Space Coast Young Republicans. CENSURE / LITIGATION FACTS (recorded candidly per brand voice): On Oct 17, 2025 Palm Bay Council voted 3-2 to censure Langevin and ask Gov. DeSantis to suspend him under FS 112.51 after Langevin posted on X 'Deport every Indian immediately,' Confederate-soldier-praise posts including a description of Robert E. Lee as 'a Godly and honorable presence,' and a request on city letterhead asking President Trump to pardon white-nationalist activist Robert Rundo (founder of the Rise Above Movement). Langevin filed a federal First Amendment suit (Langevin v. Palm Bay) represented by Anthony Sabatini; bench trial began Jan 21, 2026.

profile.background: Michael C. 'Mike' Blake is the elected at-large Mayor of the City of Cocoa, Florida (Brevard County, pop ~17K), and a lifelong Cocoa resident. He won re-election Nov 5, 2024 defeating George Allen Cowart 62.66% (5,081) to 37.34% (3,028) for a term running through the next regular city election in 2028. His tenure on Cocoa's elected body spans 17+ years: City Council 1998-2001, Mayor 2004-2012 (two terms), District 1 Councilman 2014-June 2016, and Mayor again from 2020 to present. By profession he is a retired Cocoa High School educator (retired early 2022). Outside city hall he has chaired the Cocoa Community Redevelopment Agency and Cocoa Housing Authority, led the Space Coast League of Cities, sat on the Florida Municipal Insurance Trust Board, and rose through the Florida League of Cities (FLC) ladder: 2nd Vice President 2022-23, 1st Vice President 2023-24, and FLC President 2024-25. FLC named him a Home Rule Hero in 2023, 2025, and 2026 for advocacy during legislative sessions to push back state preemption of municipal authority. Religion / family / military / education credentials are NOT FOUND in any public source reviewed.

profile.background: Wesley 'Wes' Morrison (age ~41) is a third-generation Space Coast Floridian whose grandfather managed one of Cape Canaveral's first grocery stores in 1965. Titusville High School graduate; B.A. Political Science (Business minor), Florida State University 2004-2008 (first college graduate in his family). Professionally a Solar Project Developer at Liberty Watt; SEIA member, Top 500 Solar Contractors 2015-2016. Two terms on Cape Canaveral City Council before being elected the city's 2nd-youngest Mayor in November 2021. Re-elected November 5, 2024 with 77.09% (2,477 votes) over Angela Raymond (22.91% / 736 votes); current 3-year term runs 2024-2027. Chairs the city's Community Redevelopment Agency Board. Active Christian church-volunteer life: LifePointe Ministries (non-denominational evangelical Space Coast community church) — Vision Team Leader and Men's Group Leader since 2012; CREW29 homeless / cold-night-shelter ministry involvement. Holds an Environmental Stewardship certificate from the Institute for Humane Studies (Dec 2016). Wife Christina; daughter Parker.

candidates.notes: ARCHETYPE: Republican-aligned, business-executive, 'Home Rule + Law-and-Order' local-government technocrat — affirmatively pro-police (cleared HIGH BAR via personal Coffee-with-a-Cop sponsorship + joint BCSO LE-fraud presentation + 'Law and Order' campaign platform) and demonstrably anti-state-preemption (2025 FLC Home Rule Hero), with a near-total NULL footprint on the cultural cells. Three HARD TRUE: (1) public_justice.q0 — he and wife Paula are NAMED sponsors of BCSO 'Coffee with a Cop' (per his own city bio: 'sponsoring Coffee with a Cop'), affirmatively hosted joint Council Member + BCSO LE-fraud public-safety presentation on Identity Theft/Fraud/Scams, and campaign explicitly platformed on 'Law and Order, keeping citizens safe from crime on our streets' — pattern of affirmative engagement beyond routine budget votes meets HIGH BAR; (2) refuse_state_overreach.q0 — 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award recipient explicitly recognized for advocacy DURING the 2025 Florida Legislative Session against state preemption of local authority; (3) refuse_state_overreach.q3 — same Home Rule Hero designation + active service on FLC Legislative Policy Committee and Federal Action Strike Team is by definition subsidiarity advocacy (lowest-competent-level decision-making vs. state/federal preemption). 27 NULL — quiet civility-coded transportation-and-budget-focused councilmember whose only RESOLUTE-visible profile is PJ.q0 + refuse_q0 + refuse_q3. No FALSE-cluster — zero documented deviations against the rubric on culture-war cells, but silence is not affirmation in a small ~10K coastal city where most council business is stormwater, code enforcement, and CRA millage rather than life/marriage/gender. Faction signal: voted to RETAIN City Manager Morley in the Jan 2025 3-2 firing vote (institutional/establishment side of the controversy rather than reform/firing-faction).

profile.background: Don Willis is a retired business executive and entrepreneur with 40+ years professional experience; owned Porter Willis (technology consulting) for ~14 years serving international corporations and small businesses. BA Magna Cum Laude, University of North Carolina; Executive Education, UNC Kenan-Flagler Business School; PMP-certified. Before council, served as the city's Parks and Recreation Commissioner, Vice-Chair of the Business and Economic Development Advisory Board (4 years), and on a private school's board of directors. Married to Paula Willis; three grown children, three grandchildren. Elected to Cape Canaveral City Council November 2021 (top vote share ~25.4% of 5,174 votes), re-elected November 2023 with 38.81% (~860 votes). Currently serves as Treasurer of the Space Coast League of Cities; member of the Florida League of Cities Transportation/Intergovernmental Relations/Mobility/Emergency Management Legislative Committee, Cape Canaveral Local Roads Committee, Space Coast TPO Governing Board, FDOT District 5 Central Florida Safety Strategic Plan Steering Committee, and FLC Federal Action Strike Team. Recipient of the 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award for advocacy during the 2025 Legislative Session. Personally aligned with the Brevard Republican Executive Committee (party 'R' set on basis of 2021 BREC YouTube address). Religion: NOT FOUND (served on board of an unnamed private school; denomination not specified; the LinkedIn 'Don Willis - Pastor at CrossBridge Baptist' result is a DIFFERENT person, NOT this Don Willis). Part of the Davis-Willis 2-vote minority that voted to RETAIN City Manager Todd Morley in the 3-2 January 2025 termination vote.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — Mayor of Miami. Archetype: establishment_r.
profile.background: Retired U.S. Army colonel (26 years). Director, U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services under President George W. Bush (Dec 2005-Apr 2008). CEO, Miami International Airport (2013-2017). Miami City Manager (Jan 2018-Feb 2020, under Mayor Francis Suarez). PhD International Relations, U. Miami. Cuban-born, raised Tampa. Ran for Miami Mayor in 2025 with endorsements from Trump, DeSantis, Cruz, Rick Scott; successfully sued the Miami City Commission to overturn the June 2025 ordinance postponing the election, winning at the 11th Judicial Circuit and the FL 3rd DCA; election proceeded Nov 4 + Dec 9 2025 (runoff). LOST runoff to Democrat Eileen Higgins 40.54% (15,099) to 59.46% (22,145). No current elected office.
